mission.title: opencost: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Provider implementation
mission.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Provider implementation. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current opencost deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opencost version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
helm list -n opencost 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opencost installation.

steps.title: Review opencost configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opencost configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get configmap -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opencost
```
Create a new OCI provider implementation, so OpenCost may be used to estimate costs for workloads that run in OCI. This will benefit OCI customers or hybrid cloud users of OCI, and increase usage of OpenCost.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Provider implementation
steps.description:
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) provider
---------

## What does this PR change?
* Adds a new cloud service provider implementation for OCI.

## Does this PR relate to any other PRs?
* N/A

## How will this PR impact users?
* Users running clusters on OCI will be able to access pricing data.

## Does this PR address any GitHub or Zendesk issues?
* Closes #2221 

## How was this PR tested?
* Tested on OCI with OKE and self-managed clusters.

## Does this PR require changes to documentation?
*

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get events -n opencost --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Provider implementation" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opencost installed or the issue environment reproducible.
